skill.name: schedule-with-outlook
skill.description: Use Rarefriend MCP to search Outlook contacts, manage Microsoft calendar events, schedule Teams meetings, and save relationship notes from an AI assistant.
skill.summary: Schedule Outlook meetings, find contacts, and manage relationship notes through Rarefriend MCP.
skill.author: Whitefield-Labs

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many high and medium indicators, but most are false positives from Markdown backticks, example JSON, and setup paths. The confirmed risk is medium because the skill asks users to run a third-party MCP package, store client credentials, and grant access to Microsoft contacts, calendar, notes, and email. No prompt injection attempt or confirmed malicious behavior was found in the reviewed files.

medium_findings.title: Third-Party MCP Package Requires Credentialed Access
medium_findings.description: The setup instructs users to run @rarefriend-ai/mcp with Rarefriend client credentials. This appears intentional, but it gives a third-party MCP service access to Microsoft data after user authorization.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The credential and package setup is explicit in both reviewed files. The risk is privacy and supply-chain exposure, not confirmed malicious behavior.

medium_findings.title: Authorized Tools Can Read and Modify User Scheduling Data
medium_findings.description: The skill documents actions that search contacts and events, create notes, create events, reschedule events, and cancel events. These are legitimate capabilities, but they can change user calendar and relationship data.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The listed MCP tools clearly include read and write operations. The file also tells the assistant to confirm a slot before creating meetings, which lowers but does not remove risk.

low_findings.title: Markdown Backticks Misclassified as Shell Execution
low_findings.description: Most external command findings are false positives. The backticks are Markdown code fences or inline tool names that document MCP calls and setup examples, not executable skill code.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed locations are Markdown examples and command documentation. No Ruby code or shell backtick execution exists in the files.

low_findings.title: Hidden Configuration Paths Are User Setup Instructions
low_findings.description: The hidden file findings point to Cursor and Claude configuration paths. They are instructions for users to edit MCP client configuration, not file access performed by the skill.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The paths appear only in setup documentation. No code in the reviewed files reads or writes those files automatically.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography and Reconnaissance Flags Are False Positives
low_findings.description: No evidence found of weak cryptographic algorithms or host reconnaissance. The scanner appears to flag words such as client secret, MCP, and list_connected_integrations in documentation.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed lines contain setup metadata and integration checks, not cryptographic operations. No hashing or encryption implementation appears in the files.

dangerous_patterns.title: npx Package Execution in Setup
dangerous_patterns.description: The setup asks users to run an npm package with npx -y. This is normal for MCP setup, but users should verify the package publisher before running it.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The npx command is directly documented. The package purpose matches the skill, so this is a supply-chain warning rather than a malware finding.

dangerous_patterns.title: Credential Values Stored in MCP Client Configuration
dangerous_patterns.description: The setup shows Rarefriend client ID and client secret stored in MCP client configuration. Users should treat these values as secrets and avoid sharing config files.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The credential environment variables are explicit in setup examples. This is a standard integration pattern with clear secret-handling implications.

content.value_statement: Scheduling across Outlook contacts and calendars can require many manual searches. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code through Rarefriend MCP workflows for contacts, availability, events, and notes.
actual_capabilities: Checks whether Rarefriend MCP and Microsoft integrations are connected.
Searches Outlook and Rarefriend contacts by name, company, role, email, or tag.
Finds available Microsoft calendar times before scheduling meetings.
Creates, reschedules, and cancels Microsoft calendar events through MCP tools.
Creates and searches Rarefriend notes and tags for relationship context.
Guides users through connecting Microsoft contacts, calendar, and email integrations.
limitations: Requires a configured Rarefriend MCP server and Rarefriend client credentials.
Requires Microsoft account authorization before contacts, calendar, or email tools work.
Does not edit native Microsoft contacts directly through Outlook tools.
Meeting creation depends on user confirmation and accurate timezone information.

use_cases.title: Book Client Meetings
use_cases.description: Find a contact, check shared availability, and create a Microsoft calendar event with attendees.

use_cases.title: Prepare for Weekly Planning
use_cases.description: Review upcoming Outlook events and organize the week by day, attendee, and meeting purpose.

use_cases.title: Maintain Relationship History
use_cases.description: Add notes after calls, search past notes, and tag contacts for follow-up workflows.

prompt_templates.title: Check Setup
prompt_templates.prompt: Check whether Rarefriend MCP and my Microsoft integrations are connected. Tell me what is missing before taking any action.

prompt_templates.title: Find a Contact
prompt_templates.prompt: Find Sarah Chen in my Outlook contacts and Rarefriend records. Show the source, email address, company, and recent notes if available.

prompt_templates.title: Schedule a Meeting
prompt_templates.prompt: Find 30 minutes next week with Jordan Lee for a Teams call. Check availability first, then ask me to confirm the best slot.

prompt_templates.title: Organize Follow-Ups
prompt_templates.prompt: Find everyone at Acme Corp, tag them as client, and summarize recent notes so I can plan follow-up meetings.

output_examples.input: Find time with Maya for a 45 minute Teams call next Tuesday or Wednesday.
output_examples.output: The assistant finds Maya in Microsoft Contacts, checks shared availability, and proposes open slots. It asks for confirmation before creating the event.

output_examples.input: What Outlook meetings do I have this week?
output_examples.output: The assistant returns a day-by-day meeting summary with titles, times, and attendee context when contact records are available.

output_examples.input: Add a note after my call with Sam about the renewal discussion.
output_examples.output: The assistant finds Sam, confirms the correct contact, and saves the note in Rarefriend for future relationship context.

best_practices: Confirm Rarefriend MCP connection status before searching contacts or calendars.
Ask for user confirmation before creating, moving, or canceling calendar events.
Mention the contact source when presenting search results from multiple systems.
anti_patterns: Do not create calendar events without confirming time, attendees, and timezone.
Do not expose Rarefriend client secrets in chat, logs, screenshots, or shared files.
Do not assume a contact match is correct when multiple similar names appear.

faq.question: Does this skill require Rarefriend?
faq.answer: Yes. It depends on Rarefriend MCP and Rarefriend client credentials for Outlook and relationship workflows.

faq.question: Can it schedule Microsoft Teams meetings?
faq.answer: It can create Microsoft calendar events through the documented MCP tools. Teams behavior depends on the connected Microsoft account settings.

faq.question: Can it search Outlook contacts?
faq.answer: Yes. It documents direct Microsoft contact search and broader Rarefriend contact search with notes and tags.

faq.question: Can it edit Outlook contacts?
faq.answer: No. The skill says Microsoft contacts are read-only through Outlook tools. It can update Rarefriend contact fields.

faq.question: What data can the skill access?
faq.answer: After authorization, it can use MCP tools for Microsoft contacts, calendar events, email search, notes, and tags.

faq.question: Is this skill safe to publish?
faq.answer: It is publishable with a medium-risk warning because it uses a third-party MCP package and handles Microsoft account data.
